Off Calendar Aug-04-2025 Continued To Aug-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition For Order To Set Aside Amendment Of Trust, And Invalidate Will Due To (1) Undue Influence, (2) Lack Of Testamentary Capacity, And (3) Elder Abuse; Remove Trustee; Compel Trustee To Provide All Trust Documents And Accounting; Impose Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Aug-04-2025 Continued To Aug-18-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kellie Chrisman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Anna Kanakkanatt, Petitioner.
Luisa Rubio, and Shirley Rubio, in person, in propria persona, Objectors.
Alisa Turner, via zoom, in propria persona, interested Party.
Bruce Turner, in person, in propria persona, interested Party.

Discussion was held regarding the settlement agreement, that the settlement agreement has a buy amount for Luisa Rubio and Shirley Rubio for $5,000.00 for each objector, and that the only objection was the boiler plate language. The Court took a recess to allow parties to sign the settlement agreement electronically, but the parties were unsuccessful due to phone issues downloading the documents. The matters are continued to August 18, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jun-09-2025 Continued To Aug-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Kellie Chrisman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Anna Kanakkanatt, Petitioner.

Luisa Rubio, and Shirley Rubio, in person, in propria persona, Objectors.

Alisa Turner, in person, in propria persona, interested Party.

Bruce Turner, via Zoom videoconference, in propria persona, interested Party.

The interpreter's name is Pilar Espinosa, Certification Number 330288, assisting the Objectors. The language to be interpreted is English to Spanish and Spanish to English.

At the outset, the court recalls the previous hearing and acknowledges Objectors' filings. Counsel, the Objectors, and interested Parties presented oral arguments to support their positions regarding the instant petition. Ms. Chrisman objects to the interested parties addressing the court, stating that they are not parties to this matter. Counsel's objections are overruled in open court. Ms. Chrisman offers an update regarding the results of the mediation with Bette Epstein confirming that the parties have about two (2) hours remaining with her. There is discussion regarding whether the Objectors intend to seek legal counsel to assist them and a request for a six-week continuance to allow Objectors to find counsel or legal advice. Ms. Chrisman objects to the length of the request.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Aug-04-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The parties are ordered back to mediation with Bette Epstein and are instructed to confirm a mediation date with Ms. Epstein within the next two weeks.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Verified Petition For Order To Set Aside Amendment Of Trust, And Invalidate Will Due To (1) Undue Influence, (2) Lack Of Testamentary Capacity, And (3) Elder Abuse; Remove Trustee; Compel Trustee To Provide All Trust Documents And Accounting; Impose Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Jun-09-2025 Continued To Aug-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Verified Petition For Order To Set Aside Amendment Of Trust, And Invalidate Will Due To (1) Undue Influence, (2) Lack Of Testamentary Capacity, And (3) Elder Abuse; Remove Trustee; Compel Trustee To Provide All Trust Documents And Accounting; Impose Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Continued To Jun-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kellie Chrisman for and with Anna Kanakkanatt, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Alasdair Clynes, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).
Luisa Rubio and Shirley Rubio, Objectors.
Julia Chiger, Niece (via Zoom).

Camille Taiara #301469, Court Certified Spanish Interpreter with an oath on file, assisting Luisa and Shirley Rubio.

Ms. Chrisman argued that the Rubio sisters were caregivers to Suzanne Castle, providing extensive personal care and companionship, triggering the presumption of undue influence under California law. The Rubio sisters described their close relationship with Ms. Castle, their role as caregivers, and their unawareness of any changes to her estate plan in their favor. They stated that they were not involved in any estate planning decisions and emphasized their long-term caregiving relationship and emotional bond with Ms. Castle. Ms. Chiger, the decedent's niece, argued that Ms. Castle expressed intentions to change her estate plan before her death and that the Rubio sisters had undue control over her affairs. She alleged certain actions taken after Ms. Castle's passing were improper, including the handling of personal effects and medical decisions.

The court noted the procedural challenges due to the self-represented status of the Rubio sisters and language barriers. The court declined to grant the petition, noting that while the presumption of undue influence applies under California Law, the Rubio sisters may have evidence to rebut it.

The court ordered the parties to participate in pro bono mediation. The order was signed in open court, and Ms. Chisman was instructed to obtain a copy of the signed order from the court's website. Copies of the order were provided to Luisa and Shirley Rubio.

Mediation must be completed by May 23, 2025. Parties must submit a mediation status update to the probate examiner no later than May 30, 2025.

The court continued the matter to June 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. A hearing regarding status of mediation was set for the same date. Luisa and Shirley Rubio were instructed to properly serve their objection to opposing counsel.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
